items.headline: City commissions outline priorities for next fiscal year
items.summary: Saratoga's five commissions—covering parks, planning, traffic safety, heritage preservation, and library/arts—presented their work plans for FY 2026-27. The approved priorities will guide city decisions on development, traffic improvements, and community services over the coming year.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is rolling out an amnesty program to help unlicensed businesses at local shopping centers get compliant without penalties. This could affect where you shop and which businesses operate legally in Cupertino's commercial areas.

items.headline: Capitol Dental Office opens on North Capitol Avenue
items.summary: A new dental office is opening at 585 N Capitol Av with a $500,000 investment. This represents a significant commercial addition to the Capitol neighborhood's healthcare services.

items.headline: City reviews federal housing and community development grants
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed applications for HOME and Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These federal grants fund affordable housing and community development projects that directly benefit Sunnyvale residents.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan to study land use and transportation changes
items.summary: The Planning Commission will study alternative approaches to land use and transportation in the San Antonio Road area. This study session could affect how that corridor develops and how residents move through it.

items.headline: New retail vitality policies proposed for zoning code changes
items.summary: The Commission is considering amendments to building and zoning regulations to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. These changes could affect where retail can locate, how it's designed, and what support struggling commercial areas receive.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The City Council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance on March 24, establishing new regulations for residents who keep bees. This change affects anyone in Campbell interested in urban beekeeping or already maintaining hives.

items.headline: Major zoning debate: transit-oriented development rules advance
items.summary: The council held a public hearing on Senate Bill 79, which would reshape zoning near transit areas to encourage housing and mixed-use development. Three council members recused themselves due to property conflicts, raising questions about how the controversial measure will ultimately be decided.
